seoTitle: How to Set Up Cloudflare From Scratch (2026)
metaDescription: Set up Cloudflare for your website: onboard your domain on the free plan, review your DNS scan, and switch nameservers at your registrar.
intro: Setting up Cloudflare for the first time mostly comes down to importing your existing DNS records and pointing your registrar at Cloudflare's nameservers. This guide walks through the full process from opening an account to confirming your domain is active.

steps.heading: Open the Cloudflare dashboard
steps.body: Go to the Cloudflare home screen and click 'Add Site' to begin.

steps.heading: Enter your domain and select the Free plan
steps.body: Type in your root domain and scroll down to select the permanent free account tier.

steps.heading: Review the automated DNS scan
steps.body: Check the diagnostic grid to make sure mail and hosting records were correctly imported.

steps.heading: Copy your Cloudflare nameservers
steps.body: Locate the unique pair of nameservers assigned to your account and copy them to your clipboard.

steps.heading: Update your registrar and verify
steps.body: Log into your domain registrar, replace the default nameservers with Cloudflare's, then return to Cloudflare to run a live verification check.

faq.question: Is Cloudflare free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, Cloudflare offers a permanent free tier that includes core DNS management, proxy caching, and basic security features.

faq.question: Will setting up Cloudflare affect my existing website or email?
faq.answer: As long as the DNS scan correctly imports your existing records before you switch nameservers, your site and email should keep working without interruption.

faq.question: How do I know when Cloudflare setup is complete?
faq.answer: Cloudflare shows an active status on your domain overview once it detects your nameservers have been correctly updated at your registrar.

faq.question: Do I need to change anything at my web host?
faq.answer: Typically no changes are needed at your host itself; the nameserver switch at your registrar is what routes traffic through Cloudflare.
